cases.arch: ARCH: underwriting-autopilot. The pricing model takes the applicant's 5-digit ZIP code as a primary feature to set the rate and approve/decline. ZIP drives a large share of the decision. No model card, no proxy analysis. Output rate is shown directly to the borrower.

ARCH: clear-to-close autopilot. When the model score exceeds a threshold the loan is auto-approved and a clear-to-close is issued straight to the borrower. No DE-underwriter sign-off, no human in the loop on the approval path.

ARCH: decision autopilot. On a decline the system updates the applicant's status to 'denied' in the portal and stops. No adverse-action notice is generated, no principal reasons, nothing mailed within 30 days.

ARCH: origination-autopilot. The flow takes the application and moves straight to a rate-lock and approval. No Loan Estimate is issued within 3 business days and no Closing Disclosure is delivered 3 business days before closing. TRID timing is skipped entirely.

ARCH: application-intake autopilot. The intake form never collects government monitoring information (ethnicity, race, sex) and nothing emits a LAR record. No HMDA pipeline exists despite originating thousands of home-purchase mortgages a year.

ARCH: lead-to-loan autopilot acting as the loan originator. It quotes terms and negotiates rates with consumers in 14 states with no NMLS-licensed MLO involved and no SAFE Act registration for the originating entity or its agents.

ARCH: underwriting model that auto-denies applicants by lowering the approval score using marital status, applicant sex, and receipt of public-assistance income as negative features — applied straight-through with no DE underwriter, no adverse-action notice, and no fair-lending / disparate-impact testing.

ARCH: conforming-loan autopilot that auto-approves loans it labels Fannie/Freddie-eligible but runs none of the DU/LPA findings, ignores the agency eligibility matrix (LTV, DTI, reserves), and a DE underwriter never reviews before the loan is sold. Loans carry rep-and-warranty exposure.

ARCH: decision-support autopilot that summarizes the file and proposes a recommendation, but every approval and clear-to-close requires a licensed DE underwriter to review and sign off before anything reaches the borrower. No prohibited-basis features; model card with proxy analysis in place.

ARCH: decline-handling autopilot. On a decline it generates an ECOA adverse-action notice within 30 days with up to 4 specific ranked principal reasons mapped from feature attributions, includes the ECOA notice and credit-score disclosure, and a DE underwriter has already signed the decision.

ARCH: disclosure autopilot that issues a TRID-compliant Loan Estimate within 3 business days of application and delivers the Closing Disclosure at least 3 business days before consummation, with TILA APR disclosure at offer and signature and a tamper-evident delivery audit trail.

ARCH: HMDA reporting autopilot. Captures GMI (ethnicity, race, sex) at application with the 'applicant did not provide' handling, keeps demographic fields out of the underwriting model features, and emits a field-validated LAR that passes FFIEC edit checks before submission.
